cards.back:
```
conf t
router bgp <local-ASN>            ! 1-65535 (2-byte) or 4-byte ASN
 bgp router-id <a.b.c.d>
 no auto-summary                  ! always disable
 no synchronization               ! always disable (legacy)
```

cards.front: Define an iBGP neighbor (same AS, typically loopback-to-loopback):
cards.back:
```
router bgp 65001
 neighbor <peer-loopback-ip> remote-as 65001
 neighbor <peer-loopback-ip> update-source loopback0
 neighbor <peer-loopback-ip> next-hop-self    ! on edge routers
```

cards.front: Advertise a network into BGP (must exist in routing table):
cards.back:
```
router bgp 65001
 network <network> mask <mask>
```
e.g. `network 10.0.0.0 mask 255.255.255.0`

cards.front: eBGP between routers more than 1 hop away (e.g. loopback peering across L3 path):

BGP TCP port: [...]
BGP eBGP default AD: 20
BGP iBGP default AD: 200

BGP TCP port: [...]
BGP eBGP default AD: 20
BGP iBGP default AD: 200

BGP TCP port: [...]
BGP eBGP default AD: 20
BGP iBGP default AD: 200

cards.back:
```
show ip bgp summary
```
Look at `State/PfxRcd` column — number = established (count of prefixes received). `Idle`/`Active` = broken peering.

cards.front: Verify BGP routes received from a specific peer:
cards.back:
```
show ip bgp neighbors <ip> received-routes      ! requires soft-reconfig inbound
show ip bgp neighbors <ip> routes               ! routes accepted into BGP table
show ip bgp neighbors <ip> advertised-routes
```

cards.front: See full BGP table (routes the router knows from BGP):
cards.back:
```
show ip bgp
show ip bgp <prefix>              ! drill into one prefix
```

cards.front: See BGP routes installed in routing table:
cards.back:
```
show ip route bgp
```
Code `B` = BGP route. `>` next to a path in `show ip bgp` = best path.

cards.front: Reset a BGP session without dropping it (soft reset, in-place policy reload):
cards.back:
```
clear ip bgp <peer-ip> soft in
clear ip bgp <peer-ip> soft out
clear ip bgp *  soft               ! all peers
```

cards.front: Hard reset BGP session (drops TCP, full re-converge):
cards.back:
```
clear ip bgp <peer-ip>
clear ip bgp *
```

cards.front: BGP path selection — first 3 tie-breakers (CCNA-level, more exist):
cards.back:
1. Highest **Weight** (Cisco-only, local to router)
2. Highest **Local Preference** (within AS)
3. Locally **originated** routes (network/redistribute)
Then: shortest AS_PATH, lowest Origin, lowest MED, eBGP over iBGP, lowest IGP cost to next-hop, lowest router ID.

```
debug ip bgp <peer-ip>            ! state transitions
debug ip bgp updates              ! UPDATE messages — careful, very chatty
undebug all
```
